color_profiles.name: labradorescence-blue-green
color_profiles.cause: Thin-film interference from Ca-rich and Na-rich exsolution lamellae at ~100–300 nm spacing. Shorter lamellar spacing produces blue; longer spacing shifts toward green and gold.

color_profiles.name: labradorescence-gold-copper
color_profiles.cause: Longer-spacing exsolution lamellae shifting interference toward yellow-orange wavelengths.

color_profiles.name: spectrolite
color_profiles.cause: Full-range lamellar spacing distribution on a very dark ground mass, producing all visible colors.

color_profiles.name: transparent-gem
color_profiles.cause: Body color from iron or other trace impurities; transparent facetable material.

visual_notes: The labradorescence appears as a floating, depth-inhabiting iridescence that shifts with viewing angle and light direction. Unlike surface iridescence, it appears to come from within the stone. The dark gray to near-black base color intensifies the color display. Best specimens show full-spectrum color shift across a single face.

optical_effects.iridescence: Labradorescence: intense thin-film interference iridescence showing blue, green, gold, copper, orange — and in spectrolite, the full visible spectrum — from exsolution lamellae oriented parallel to {010}. The effect is directional and changes with both viewing angle and light angle.
optical_effects.play_of_color: Labradorescence: intense thin-film interference iridescence showing blue, green, gold, copper, orange — and in spectrolite, the full visible spectrum — from exsolution lamellae oriented parallel to {010}. The effect is directional and changes with both viewing angle and light angle.
